source: Claude gap analysis 2026-06-11 (Track D — 'make the model smarter' lever)
title: Tiered model escalation: hard tasks get the premium tier, chat stays cheap
current_state: Root rides Sonnet for everything; normie rides free 70B for everything. Task difficulty never influences model choice. The budget cap and 'be as smart as Claude Code' are in unmanaged tension — the router has tiers but nothing uses 'premium'.
target_state: Per-TASK escalation: code-edit turns (a tool loop that touched modify_file/create_file), architecture questions, and explicit 'think hard' requests route tier='premium' (e.g. claude-opus-4-8 or latest) under a separate small daily premium budget (default $1/day, config-guarded); everything else unchanged. Escalation is logged in evolution.jsonl so retro.py can show whether premium turns earned their cost.
migration_plan: 1. llm_router: premium tier definition + its own TPD/cost sub-budget with brownout to default tier.
2. Escalation signal: explicit user marker ('think hard', 'ultra') OR heuristic (code-shaped + multi-file).
3. app/config.py PREMIUM_DAILY_LIMIT — config edit requires Ash's explicit go (rule 1); ship default OFF (0.0) so the merge is inert until he flips it.
4. Tests: escalation routes premium; budget exhaustion falls back; OFF default routes nothing.
risk_notes: app/config.py is FORBIDDEN without explicit instruction — implement around it, default-off, and hand Ash the one-line enable.
